books.title: The Age of Fable
books.author: Thomas Bulfinch
books.rationale: The Age of Fable contains mythological stories primarily from Greece and Rome, although it also includes some stories from Norse and Eastern traditions. This is a stretch book for Grades 5-6 as the reading level is more challenging than the mythology stories assigned in the earlier grades.

books.title: Russian Fairy Tales
books.author: Alexander Afanasyev
books.rationale: Pushkin, Afanasyev, and many others wrote and collected Russian folk tales in the 19th century. Students read a few tales in the lesson plans and may enjoy the others on their own time.

books.title: South American Jungle Tales
books.author: Horacio Quiroga
books.rationale: A Uruguayan author and playwright who lived at the end of the 19th century, Quiroga is sometimes compared to Rudyard Kipling and Edgar Allen Poe.

books.title: Short Stories & Folktales
books.author: Various
books.rationale: Lesson plans curate and include additional folk tales and shorter writings from 19th century authors such as Tolstoy and George Macdonald.

books.title: A Christmas Carol
books.author: Charles Dickens
books.rationale: As a famous work of 19th century literature, A Christmas Carol expands students' understanding of this historical period while also nourishing them with a thought-provoking tale about greed and generosity. It is scheduled in the mythology stream as Dicken's work has become a modern myth, shaping our conceptions of Christmas and more.
